summary: Iter 78 backend testing — Iter 77d + 77e changes. Created test_iter78_weekoff_and_grid.py with 12 tests: 6 direct compute_textile_day unit tests (Iter 77d weekly-off rules), 5 _validate_policy boundary tests for week_off_min_working_hours (0-16), and 1 HTTP integration test on /admin/attendance/monthly-grid. Result: 11/12 PASS; 1 FAIL uncovers a real spec-violation bug — on Policy 1 week-off days the standard-hours cap (ot_allowed=False → 8h) fires BEFORE the week-off branch, so a 10h week-off punch is capped to 8h instead of the spec-mandated 'uncapped, no OT restriction on week-off'. All 13 pre-existing iter77 regression tests still PASS. Cleanup script ran; 2 test firms + 2 test users + 12 attendance records removed.

critical.issue: Policy 1 week-off day gets duty capped at standard_working_hours (8h) when attendance_policy_override.ot_allowed=False, even though the review spec (item B.2) explicitly requires 'hours=10 (uncapped, no OT restriction on week-off)'. Root cause: in server.py:1261-1267 the `max_hours = 24.0 if ot_applicable_user else standard_hrs` cap is applied unconditionally BEFORE the `is_weekly_off` branch (line 1284+), so a 10h week-off shift with ot_allowed=False is capped to 8h. Observed cell: {'raw_hours':10.0,'hours':8.0,'present':1.0,'weekly_off':True}. Fix: skip / bypass the OT cap when `is_weekly_off is True` in the Policy 1 branch (or use 24h cap on week-off regardless of ot_allowed).

action_items: In server.py compute_textile_day (Policy 1 branch, ~line 1261), skip the OT cap (`max_hours = standard_hrs when ot_allowed=False`) when `is_weekly_off is True`. On week-off days there is no OT restriction per the spec — duty should carry the full worked hours (still bounded by 24h absolute maximum). After fix, the week-off cell for the integration test should return hours=10.0 (matching raw_hours) instead of 8.0.
Consider adding a dedicated regression unit test covering: Policy 1 + weekly_off + ot_allowed=False + duty=10h → duty_hours should be 10.0 (not 8.0), present=1.0, full_day_pay_weekoff=True.
critical_code_review_comments: compute_textile_day (server.py ~L1261): the OT cap runs before the week-off branch. This inverts priority — on a week-off day the employee is by definition working beyond the standard schedule; capping to standard_hrs there defeats the point of `full_day_pay_weekoff`. Recommend `max_hours = 24.0 if (ot_applicable_user or is_weekly_off) else standard_hrs`.
server.py is 15,141 lines — well beyond the 700-line guideline. Splitting the attendance/policy/salary sections into dedicated modules would materially improve maintainability.

Field naming inconsistency: the docstring for the Iter 77d rules uses `week_off_full_day_user` (line 1183) which reads user['week_off_full_day']. Consider consolidating naming to reduce cognitive load.
seed_data_creation: Test fixture created 1 firm (Iter78-WeekOff-*) + 1 employee (TEST_Iter78_*) + 4 manual punches (2 workday, 2 weekly-off). All removed by cleanup_test_data.py --apply (2 firms + 2 users + 12 attendance docs deleted; the 2 iter77 leftovers from prior runs were also cleaned).
context_for_next_testing_agent: After the main agent fixes the week-off cap bug, re-run only the failing test: `EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL=https://emplo-connect-1.preview.emergentagent.com pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iter78_weekoff_and_grid.py::test_monthly_grid_returns_raw_and_policy_adjusted_hours -v`. Also re-run the full iter77 suite to confirm no regression: `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iter77_policy1_cap.py -v`. Prefix any new temp data with Iter78-/TEST_Iter78_.
rca_of_issue:
RCA for FAIL — `test_monthly_grid_returns_raw_and_policy_adjusted_hours`:

Reproduction:
  1. Create Policy 1 firm with attendance_policy.week_off_min_working_hours=8, weekly_off_days=[6] (Sunday).
  2. Create employee, set attendance_policy_override.ot_allowed=False.
  3. Post 12h of punches on a Saturday (workday) and 10h of punches on a Sunday (week-off).
  4. GET /api/admin/attendance/monthly-grid/{cid}/2026-01?from_date=SAT&to_date=SUN.
  5. Inspect the Sunday cell.

Expected (per review spec B.2 & scenario 3): hours=10.0 (uncapped, no OT restriction on week-off), raw_hours=10.0, present=1.0, weekly_off=True.
Actual: hours=8.0, raw_hours=10.0, present=1.0, weekly_off=True — duty capped at standard_working_hours.

Root cause: In server.py compute_textile_day, lines 1261-1267 compute `max_hours = 24.0 if ot_applicable_user else standard_hrs` and cap duty_hrs to that value BEFORE the `is_weekly_off` branch at line 1284. Because the employee's override sets ot_allowed=False, standard_hrs=8.0 is chosen and 10h is trimmed to 8h. The week-off branch then correctly assigns present_days=1.0 and full_day_pay_weekoff=True — but the hours field has already been mutilated.

Mitigation: change line 1261 to `max_hours = 24.0 if (ot_applicable_user or is_weekly_off) else standard_hrs`. This preserves the 24h absolute cap on all days, honours OT-off on regular workdays, and lets week-off duty flow through uncapped (still bounded by 24h). No other test scenarios are impacted (the iter77 suite exercises the workday path only for ot_allowed=False; the week-off scenarios in iter77 use the default ot_allowed=True where max_hours=24 already).
